summary: GitHub issue planning — analyze repos, plan work, create and track issues.

entry.use_when: Starting any planning, analysis, or issue-management workflow

subcommands.use_when: User wants to see what is already tracked
User wants to draft or submit a single issue quickly
User wants to build or refresh project docs before planning
First-time setup or re-configuring the GitHub repo
GitHub authentication is failing or needs to be refreshed
User wants to clear cached data and free Claude context after planning
User wants to view or change planning preferences (milestone_assign, label_auto_assign, confirm_arch_changes)
Quick snapshot of what caches and docs are currently loaded

unload_policy.description: Per-command cache lifecycle policy. Each command entry declares unload[] (caches to clear when done) and keep[] (caches to preserve). Call apply_unload_policy(command) to execute. Persistent state (issues, project docs, config.yaml, .env) is never touched by any policy.
